title: BigBear.ai Q1 2026: Strong Margins Offset Revenue Disappointment
text: BigBear.ai (NYSE:BBAI) reported Q1 2026 results on May 5, delivering a mixed financial picture. While the company missed analyst expectations on both the top and bottom lines, it achieved remarkable profitability improvements. Revenue came in at $34.4 million, falling short of the projected $35.28 million and essentially flat compared to Q1 2025's $34.8 million. The earnings per share loss of -$0.12 exceeded the anticipated -$0.08 loss. However, the standout metric was gross margin expansion. The company achieved a gross margin of 34.0% in Q1 2026, representing a remarkable 1,270 basis point expansion from 21.3% in the prior year quarter. Management attributed this dramatic improvement to a higher mix of Generative AI revenue. Despite near-term revenue challenges, BigBear.ai secured significant contract wins, including a $53 million sole source classified contract with an Intelligence Community customer and a $7 million contract covering Chicago O'Hare Airport and Dallas Fort Worth Airport. The company's funded backlog surged 44% quarter-over-quarter to $79.1 million, while total backlog grew to $281.9 million, suggesting improving revenue visibility ahead. Cash and investments stood at $431.5 million against minimal debt of just $16.6 million, providing substantial financial flexibility for strategic pursuits.
